Level 1: Bioinformatics workflow applied to transcriptomics Project definition Recommended pre-requisites Technical requirements Objectives

Level 1: Bioinformatics workflow applied to transcriptomics Anya Greenberg 2021-08-17 Project definition Recommended pre-requisites Basic understanding of genetics Beginner experience with R, Python, or an equivalent statistical language Technical requirements 8 GB RAM 5 GB free disk space If you dont meet the requirements above, contact @anya on the STEM-Away forums. Objectives Learn about the important concepts of bioinformatics spanning computer science, statistics, and biology. Understand a generalized workflow for transcriptomic analysis of microarray data. Implement workflow in intern-led research project. Technical skills objectives Learn about important concepts of bioinformatics Computer science Data structures Data analysis and visualization Functions and abstraction Introduction to statistics for bioinformatics Hypothesis testing Clustering and similariyt metrics Introduction to transcriptomics Application of generalized workflow for transcriptomic analysis Data acquisition from NCBIs Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) Quality control - inter-array vs. intra-array Differential gene expression (using linear modeling) Computational functional analysis Enrichment Over-representation Pathway/ networks Use R programming and other bioinformatic tools in transcriptomic research R syntax, pacakges, documentation, and troubleshooting Database for Annotation, Visualization, and Integrated Discover (DAVID) Enrichr Metascape: a gene anotation and analysis resource STRING: functional protein association networks Gene Expression Profiling Interactive Analysis (GEPIA) Research practices Critical thinking Literature searches Telling a story Soft skills objectives Project and time management Virtual collaboration and teamwork Technical and project presentations Leadership and mentoriing (student leads)

Module 1 Introduction, part 1: Understanding the basics 1.1 R programming and RStudio 1.1.1 Installing R and RStudio R is a free software environment for statistical computing and graphics. Find out more at the R project site. RStudio is an integrated development environment (IDE*) for R. Find out more at the RStudio site. 1.1.1.1 Instructions Download Rv4 (as of August 2021) Select mirror* site that is closest to your geographic location. Select appropriate link for OS (Linux, MacOS, Windows). Download base, run and follow the instructions. If on windows, download Rtools, run and follow the instructions. Download RStudio Desktop. Yay! Youre ready to start programming! Open RStudio and install the tidyverse package. Load the tidyverse library. HINT: You will need the functions install.packages() and library(). If you type ?function_name() into the console panel of RStudio and hit enter, you will be able to see the docuumentation of the function in the help panel. This documentation will tell you the inputs and outputs for that function. At the bottom of the help panel will be a few examples of the functions usage. More information on R documentation can be found [TODO]. 1.2 Math concepts for bioinformatics 1.3 NCBI resources: PubMed and GEO
